name: Acetyl-L-Carnitine HCl
historicalUse: L-Carnitine was isolated from meat in 1905. Its acetylated form, acetyl-L-carnitine (ALCAR), was identified and characterized in the late 20th century. Classical medicine did not isolate this compound, but the broader concept of brain-nourishing foods and preparations — particularly meat broths and organ tissues rich in carnitine — appears in Arabic medical nutrition literature. Ibn Sina's recommendations for brain-supportive diets emphasized nutrient-dense animal foods consistent with high carnitine content.
classicalSources: Ibn Sina — dietary recommendations for cognitive health in Canon of Medicine
modernEvidence: ALCAR crosses the blood-brain barrier and acts as an acetyl group donor for acetylcholine synthesis, supporting cholinergic neurotransmission. Multiple RCTs demonstrate improvement in cognitive function, memory, and mood in age-related cognitive decline, mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and diabetic neuropathy. It also supports mitochondrial fatty acid transport and has neuroprotective antioxidant properties.
mechanisms: Acetylcholine precursor — donates acetyl group for ACh synthesis in cholinergic neurons
Mitochondrial fatty acid transport — facilitates long-chain fatty acid entry into mitochondria
Neuroprotection — reduces oxidative stress and apoptosis in neuronal tissue
NGF sensitization — enhances neuronal response to nerve growth factor
Myelination support — contributes to myelin sheath lipid metabolism
benefits: Supports cognitive function and mental clarity
safetyProfile: Well tolerated. Mild GI discomfort in some individuals. Fishy odor in urine at high doses is benign and due to trimethylamine metabolite.
contraindications: Hypothyroidism — may theoretically affect thyroid hormone metabolism at high doses
Seizure disorder — case reports of increased seizure activity; use with caution
drugInteractions: Warfarin — case reports of increased anticoagulant effect; monitor INR
physicianNotes: Clinically appropriate in age-related cognitive decline, MCI, and diabetic neuropathy. Cholinergic mechanism means caution in patients on anticholinergic medications (opposing effects). Monitor INR in anticoagulated patients. Therapeutic dose range 500–2000mg/day.
patientGuidance: Best taken in the morning or early afternoon due to potential energizing effects. A harmless fishy odor in urine may occur — this is normal. If you take blood thinners, inform your physician.
